# Το DreamLab δίνει τη δυνατότητα στο Imperial College London να επιταχύνει την έρευνα φαρμάκων και υπερτροφών που πιθανόν μπορούν να βοηθήσουν όσους νοσούν από COVID-19.

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=T%CE%BF%20DreamLab%20%20%CE%B5%CF%80%CE%B9%CF%84%CE%B1%CF%87%CF%8D%CE%BD%CE%B5%CE%B9%20%CF%84%CE%B7%CE%BD%20%CE%AD%CF%81%CE%B5%CF%85%CE%BD%CE%B1%20%CF%86%CE%B1%CF%81%CE%BC%CE%AC%CE%BA%CF%89%CE%BD%20%CE%BA%CE%B1%CE%B9%20%CF%85%CF%80%CE%B5%CF%81%CF%84%CF%81%CE%BF%CF%86%CF%8E%CE%BD%20%CF%80%CE%BF%CF%85%20%CF%80%CE%B9%CE%B8%CE%B1%CE%BD%CF%8C%CE%BD%20%CE%BC%CF%80%CE%BF%CF%81%CE%BF%CF%8D%CE%BD%20%CE%BD%CE%B1%20%CE%B2%CE%BF%CE%B7%CE%B8%CE%AE%CF%83%CE%BF%CF%85%CE%BD%20%CF%8C%CF%83%CE%BF%CF%85%CF%82%20%CE%BD%CE%BF%CF%83%CE%BF%CF%8D%CE%BD%20%CE%B1%CF%80%CF%8C%20COVID-19&body=

3 minute read

Χάρη στους χρήστες του DreamLab σε όλο τον κόσμο, το Imperial College London ολοκλήρωσε τη Φάση 1 του ερευνητικού του έργου Corona-AI σε μόλις έξι μήνες. Επίτευγμα που ολοκληρώθηκε σημαντικά πιο γρήγορα από ό, τι θα ήταν δυνατό να ολοκληρωθεί με τυπικές μεθόδους έρευνας.

Επιστήμονες που εργάζονται στο έργο του Imperial College London ανακοίνωσαν πρώιμα ευρήματα από την έρευνα, συμπεριλαμβάνοντας την ταυτοποίηση μορίων με αντι-ιικές ιδιότητες σε καθημερινές φυτικές τροφές όπως τα μούρα (ιδιαίτερα φραγκοστάφυλα, cranberries και βατόμουρα), τα μήλα, τα πορτοκάλια, τα λεμόνια το λάχανο, το μπρόκολο, τα εσπεριδοειδή, τα κρεμμύδια, το σκόρδο, ο μαϊντανός και τα φασόλια.

Η έρευνα ανακάλυψε επίσης, ότι τα κοινά φάρμακα που χρησιμοποιούνται για την καταπολέμηση των καρδιαγγειακών και μεταβολικών διαταραχών \- όπως η σιμβαστατίνη, η ατορβαστατίνη και η μετφορμίνη \- θα μπορούσαν ενδεχομένως να «επανατοποθετηθούν» για την αντιμετώπιση του COVID-19. Τα ευρήματα θα αναλυθούν τώρα περαιτέρω από τους ερευνητές, οι οποίοι υποστηρίζουν ότι απαιτείται κλινική επικύρωση για να εκτιμηθεί τι αντίκτυπο μπορεί να έχουν αυτά τα μόρια.

Ο Δρ. Kirill Veselkov από το Τμήμα Χειρουργικής και Καρκίνου στο Imperial College του Λονδίνου, ο οποίος ηγείται του έργου Corona-AI, αναφέρει: «Ενώ υπάρχει, ορθώς, μεγάλη εστίαση στην εξεύρεση εμβολίου για τον COVID-19, αναζητούμε τρόπους για να βοηθήσουμε δυνητικά τους ανθρώπους όταν έχουν προσβληθεί από τον ιό, μειώνοντας είτε τη διάρκεια είτε τη σοβαρότητα της νόσου.»

«Για ασθενείς που μπορεί να μην χρειάζεται να νοσηλευτούν, υπάρχει σημαντική ανάγκη για καινοτόμο και οικονομικά αποδοτική περίθαλψη εκτός νοσοκομείου. Αναμένουμε ότι οι στρατηγικές διατροφής ακριβείας \- σχεδιασμένες με πλούσιες σε φυτοχημικά υπερτροφές \- μπορούν να προσφέρουν μια νέα λύση σε αυτό το θέμα."

**Βοηθώντας στην αντιμετώπιση του COVID-19**

Το ερευνητικό έργο Corona-AI του Imperial College London πραγματοποιήθηκε χάρη στους χρήστες του DreamLab σε όλο τον κόσμο.

Το DreamLab λειτουργεί χρησιμοποιώντας την συλλογική επεξεργαστική δύναμη φόρτισης των smartphones, για να δημιουργήσει έναν εικονικό υπερυπολογιστή ικανό να επεξεργάζεται πολλές χιλιάδες υπολογισμούς, επιταχύνοντας τον χρόνο που απαιτείται για την απόδοση αποτελεσμάτων.

Από τον Απρίλιο 2020, οι χρήστες του DreamLab είχαν την επιλογή να ενεργοποιήσουν είτε το ερευνητικό έργο του Imperial για τον καρκίνο, το Project DRUGS (Phase 4), διερευνώντας συνδέσεις μεταξύ συγκεκριμένων φαρμάκων, μορίων τροφών και γονιδιωματικών δικτύων καρκίνου, είτε το ερευνητικό έργο Corona-AI Phase 1. Μέχρι στιγμής, η εφαρμογή DreamLab έχει ληφθεί από σχεδόν 1 εκατομμύριο χρήστες smartphone σε 17 χώρες για να επιταχυνθεί η επιστημονική έρευνα.

Ο Andrew Dunnett, Διευθυντής Ομίλου Vodafone, SDGs, Sustainable Business and Foundations, δήλωσε: «Καθώς ο COVID-19 συνεχίζει να προκαλεί την κοινωνία μας με πρωτοφανείς τρόπους, η εφαρμογή DreamLab έχει ενεργοποιήσει μια παγκόσμια κοινότητα εθελοντών - από πελάτες της Vodafone μέχρι χρήστες άλλων δικτύων - για να κάνουν τη διαφορά. Είναι ένα παράδειγμα έμπνευσης από τους ανθρώπους και την τεχνολογία που ενώνουν τις δυνάμεις τους για να κάνουν τον κόσμο μας ένα καλύτερο μέρος για όλους.»

Κατά την διάρκεια της πρώτης φάσης, το ερευνητικό έργο Corona-AI εξέτασε χιλιάδες υπάρχοντα φάρμακα και μόρια τροφών, χρησιμοποιώντας την Τεχνητή Νοημοσύνη για να ολοκληρώσει 100 εκατομμύρια μαθηματικούς υπολογισμούς και να βοηθήσει τους επιστήμονες να διερευνήσουν την πιθανή επίδρασή τους για τον COVID-19.

Όταν όλες οι φάσεις της έρευνας ολοκληρωθούν \- εκτιμάται τον Δεκέμβριο του 2021 - τα αποτελέσματα θα τεθούν στη διάθεση της ιατρικής κοινότητας για τη διευκόλυνση των κλινικών δοκιμών. Τα ευρήματα που σχετίζονται με τις τροφές θα μπορούσαν ενδεχομένως να μεταφραστούν σε διατροφικές συμβουλές για ασθενείς που αναρρώνουν από τον COVID-19.

Για περισσότερες πληροφορίες σχετικά με το DreamLab και το έργο Corona-AI, επισκεφθείτε την σελίδα [

# Covid-19: DreamLab voltooit fase 1 van zijn Corona-AI-project

17 Sep 2020

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=DreamLab%20voltooit%20fase%201%20van%20zijn%20Corona-AI-project&body=

2 minute read

Landen over de hele wereld komen langzaamaan uit hun coronavirus-lockdowns, maar de dreiging van de wereldwijde pandemie is nog lang niet voorbij.

Wetenschappers onderzoeken elke mogelijke behandeling om Covid-19 te bestrijden en dat is waar je smartphone om de hoek komt kijken. Met de DreamLab-app van Vodafone Foundation kan je telefoon dit belangrijke werk drastisch versnellen.

DreamLab gebruikt de collectieve rekenkracht van inactieve smartphones om complexe gegevens te analyseren, waardoor essentieel onderzoek voor een team van wetenschappers van Imperial College London wordt versneld. Een desktopcomputer die 24 uur per dag draait, zou tientallen jaren nodig hebben om de gegevens te verwerken, maar een netwerk van 100.000 smartphones die 's nachts draaien, kan het werk in slechts een paar maanden doen.

Dankzij onze DreamLab-gebruikers is fase 1 van het Corona-AI-project nu voltooid. Sinds de lancering van het project in april hebben onze DreamLab-gebruikers bijna 100 miljoen berekeningen gemaakt.

De snelheid en schaal van dit onderzoek is mogelijk gemaakt met ongeveer 140.000 apparaten die elke nacht ongeveer 1,3 miljoen berekeningen uitvoeren met DreamLab. Dat zijn ongeveer 10 berekeningen per nacht per apparaat, met de gecombineerde verwerkingskracht van DreamLab een paar keer sneller dan de eigen supercomputer van Imperial.

Corona-AI fase 1 – 100 miljoen berekeningen

140.000 telefoons voeren elke nacht 1,3 miljoen berekeningen uit

140.000 telefoons = drie supercomputers

Deze fase was gericht op het onderzoeken van duizenden bestaande medicijnen en voedselmoleculen en het identificeren van die met antivirale eigenschappen, die zouden kunnen worden gebruikt om patiënten met coronavirus te behandelen.

Het project maakt gebruik van kunstmatige intelligentie (AI) om te begrijpen welke effecten bestaande medicijnen en voedselmoleculen kunnen hebben op het genetische profiel van Covid-19 in wiskundige simulaties. De AI selecteert welke medicijnen en voedingsmiddelen moeten worden onderzocht op basis van inzichten van onderzoekers en ook op basis van de berekeningen waarvan de kans het grootst is.

Nu fase 1 van het Corona-AI-project is voltooid, zal Imperial College deze eerste bevindingen publiceren en vervolgens begint fase 2. Terwijl fase 1 keek naar de effecten van individuele medicijnen en voedselmoleculen op Covid-19, zal fase 2 kijken naar het optimaliseren van combinaties van deze medicijnen en voedingsmiddelen voor een betere efficiëntie tegen de ziekte.

Bij beide fasen is het aan onderzoekers in het laboratorium om alle veelbelovende bevindingen uit de wiskundige simulaties van Corona-AI in de echte wereld te repliceren. Alleen dan kunnen behandelingen verder worden ontwikkeld.

Dr. Kirill Veselkov, de hoofdwetenschapper van het project aan Imperial College London, benadrukte het belang van het ontwikkelen van behandelingen, zelfs als er een effectief vaccin wordt ontwikkeld.

"Alle mogelijke behandelingen die uit dit project worden ontwikkeld, zouden een vaccin aanvullen, niet vervangen", zei dr. Veselkov. "Vooral als het virus muteert en zich aanpast, blijft het de komende jaren bij ons".

Dit werk wordt mogelijk gemaakt als we allemaal onze smartphones (en tablets) elke nacht aansluiten en de DreamLab-app van stroom voorzien. De app is beschikbaar voor iOS en Android en kan gratis worden gedownload in de [App Store]( en [Google Play](

# How Vodafone is improving energy efficiency

Telecoms is an energy intensive business.   We are managing the significant challenge of running and expanding our mobile and fixed networks, fulfilling our customers’ growing demands for data while meeting our commitments to reduce carbon emissions.

Our services can help our customers and businesses of all sizes reduce their own energy consumption.  And we believe that our digital technology can help society to become more energy efficient and greener, increasing the use of renewable power and transforming national and local energy systems for the future.

#### Improving our energy efficiency

The expansion of our mobile and fixed networks means we now carry around 10 times more data compared to just five years ago, yet our total power consumption has remained roughly flat over the same period.  Every gigabyte of data carried on our network today consumes 90% less power than it did in 2017.

Vodafone’s mobile base stations, fixed access sites and technology centres account for around 96% of our total energy consumption, with the rest used by our shops, offices, and other activities.  To improve our energy efficiency, we therefore focus on the areas of our business with the greatest potential for efficiency gains.

This includes modernising our networks, upgrading power-hungry 3G equipment to more efficient 4G/5G standards, and consolidating our mast sites.  Our data centres and network sites are being equipped with the latest high-performance servers and storage, backed by highly efficient passive infrastructure for power conversion and cooling.

By retiring our legacy 3G networks, we are freeing up spectrum for 4G and 5G services that support greater volumes of data and increase our energy efficiency.  Vodafone has achieved significant energy savings in four European markets by reallocating our 3G spectrum and we expect to complete this transformation across Europe by 2024.

We are installing new 5G technologies, such as Massive-MIMO operating on the 2600 MHz spectrum band.  Massive-MIMO dramatically increases our mobile data capacity compared to 3G and 4G, for a relatively small increase in overall power consumption.  5G Massive MIMO is around 15x more energy efficient than 3G and 3x more efficient than 4G to deliver the same quantity of data.

Vodafone is rolling out ‘multi-band’ technology that can accommodate multiple frequency bands in a single antenna, replacing legacy antennae that only support one band.  On frequencies that are upgraded with multi-band equipment, we estimate that energy consumption will be reduced by 30%.

Vodafone is also exploring ways of reducing the power used by mobile networks during times of low traffic demand, without impacting our service.  We are deploying advanced software that optimises radio power, including automatically switching off some capacity at night and at certain times during the day.

We have implemented sophisticated machine learning algorithms to optimise cooling in our technology centres, and our ‘big data’ analytics system continually monitors data from electricity suppliers and smart meters at over 53,000 locations across Europe and Egypt.  We are virtualising software applications and moving to cloud solutions, allowing us to shut down less efficient legacy server infrastructure.

11 Vodafone markets have already adopted the ‘best in class’ ISO 50001 Energy Management Standard in their operations.  We engage with suppliers to explore energy efficiency improvements for both hardware and software, benchmarking them for energy efficiency.

Despite the ever-growing demand for data and the expansion of our networks, our energy efficiency drive reduced our total Scope 1 and 2 greenhouse gas emissions in FY22 by 23% to 1.09 million tonnes of CO2e.

#### Helping businesses improve their energy efficiency

In 2020, Vodafone committed to helping our business customers reduce their own carbon emissions by a cumulative total of 350 million tonnes globally over 10 years between 2020 and 2030.

Since setting this target, Vodafone has helped customers improve their energy efficiency and saved an estimated 22.7 million tonnes of carbon emissions. Vodafone’s IoT service offer - including logistics, fleet management and smart metering - has been central in delivering these savings so far.

We are providing free advice and support to SMEs through our V-Hub service, with the aim of reaching 5 million SMEs.  V-Hub provides content on helping small businesses use digitalisation to improve their energy efficiency.

#### Helping the greening of society

There are enormous opportunities for digital technology to help governments transform their energy systems for the future, and we believe that digitalising national and local energy systems is critical if society is to move towards renewables and improve energy efficiency.

Digital technology can provide real-time data on energy production and consumption, helping to create smarter and more flexible energy grids that better match demand with supply.  Combined with automation and the internet of things (IoT), technology solutions can deliver a more intelligent, convenient, and cost-effective use of energy.

Opportunities include more widespread adoption of consumer devices that measure and improve energy consumption to city-wide smart energy management systems that manage street lighting and make buildings more efficient.

To deliver the EU’s twin digital and green vision, it will require sustained investment in technology innovation, driven by collaboration between public and private sectors, and across industries.

Across Europe, Vodafone has held showcases and exhibitions to show the potential of digital technology to green our society, demonstrating how we can boost green jobs, grow green technology skills and accelerate the wider green economy.

We’re playing our part by investing in a range of green technologies such as smart metering, IoT energy management solutions and smart cities solutions.  We continue to look for opportunities to roll these solutions out at scale across Europe.

# COVID-19: DreamLab completa a 1ª Fase do projeto Corona-AI

17 Sep 2020

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=DreamLab%20completa%20a%201%C2%AA%20Fase%20do%20projeto%20Corona-AI&body=

3 minute read

Vários países ao redor do Mundo têm vindo a emergir lentamente do confinamento resultante desta pandemia global que está longe de terminar.

Os cientistas estão a investigar possíveis tratamentos para combater a Covid-19 e o seu smartphone fez a diferença neste processo. Através da App da Fundação Vodafone, DreamLab, o seu telemóvel contribuiu para acelerar significativamente este trabalho importante de investigação.

O DreamLab utiliza o poder de processamento coletivo dos smartphones para analisar dados complexos, contribuindo para acelerar investigações vitais desenvolvidas por uma equipa de cientistas da Imperial College London. Um computador fixo, funcionando 24 horas por dia, demoraria várias décadas a processar todos os dados, no entanto uma rede de 100.000 smartphones, funcionando apenas durante a noite, pode realizar esse mesmo processamento em apenas alguns meses.

Graças aos utilizadores da App DreamLab, a 1ª Fase do projeto Corona-AI está completa. Desde o lançamento do projeto em abril, os nossos utilizadores permitiram a análise de aproximadamente 100 milhões de cálculos.

A rapidez e a evolução desta investigação foram possíveis porque cerca de 140.000 dispositivos completaram aproximadamente 1,3 milhões de cálculos por noite, através da App DreamLab. Em média, cada dispositivo processou 10 cálculos por noite, através do poder de processamento combinado do DreamLab, mais rápido que o do próprio supercomputador da Imperial College London.

1ª Fase do projeto Corona-AI – 100 milhões de cálculos efetuados

140.000 smartphones que processaram 1,3 milhões de cálculos por noite

140.000 smartphones = 3 supercomputadores

Esta fase de investigação focou-se em examinar milhares de medicamentos existentes no mercado e moléculas de alimentos, identificando quais os que apresentam propriedades antivirais que possam ser usadas para tratar pacientes com Coronavírus.

Este projeto usa Inteligência Artificial com o intuito de compreender, em simulações matemáticas, quais os efeitos que certos medicamentos existentes no mercado e moléculas de alimentos poderão ter no perfil genético da Covid-19. A Inteligência Artificial seleciona os medicamentos e alimentos a examinar, através de cálculos que permitem identificar quais os mais prováveis de atingir o sucesso ao nível da investigação e através de insights de cientistas de investigação.

A 1ª Fase do projeto Corona-AI está completa e a Imperial College London irá publicar os resultados iniciais da sua investigação. Posteriormente, a 2ª Fase deste projeto irá iniciar-se. Enquanto que a 1ª Fase se focou nos efeitos de medicamentos e moléculas de alimentos no perfil genético da Covid-19, de forma isolada, a 2ª Fase irá otimizar combinações desses medicamentos e moléculas para melhorar a eficiência no combate à doença.

Com ambas as fases de investigação, caberá aos investigadores, em laboratório, replicar quaisquer descobertas promissoras ao nível das simulações matemáticas resultantes projeto Corona-AI. Só então, qualquer tratamento poderá ser desenvolvido mais ao detalhe.

O Dr. Kirill Veselkov, cientista responsável pelo projeto e respetiva equipa de investigação na Imperial College London, enfatizou a importância do desenvolvimento de tratamentos, mesmo que seja desenvolvida uma vacina eficaz.

“Quaisquer potenciais tratamentos desenvolvidos a partir deste projeto complementariam uma vacina, não iriam substituí-la,” refere o Dr. Veselkov. “Especialmente se o vírus sofrer mutações e se adaptar, permanecendo connosco nos próximos anos”.

Este trabalho será possível se todos ativarmos a App DreamLab nos nossos smartphones todas as noites. Esta App está disponível para iOS e Android, podendo fazer o download grátis na [App Store]( e [Google Play](

# Cell Identity Hunter Phase 2 - Romania

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=Cell%20Identity%20Hunter%20Phase%202%20-%20Romania&body=

1 minute read

### Ce se va realiza prin faza 2 a Cell Identity Hunter?

Îți mulțumim pentru susținerea fazei 2 a Cell Identity Hunter! Obiectivul acestui proiect este de a caracteriza ecosistemul tumorii, în mod specific relația dintre sistemul imunitar și celulele tumorale. Acest lucru este posibil datorită combinației între trei abordări tehnologice de ultimă oră de către Asociația Italiană pentru Cercetarea Cancerului (AIRC):

1. Echipa AIRC va cartografia ecosistemul tumorii determinând natura fiecăreia dintre celulele sale. Se va face acest lucru prin adoptarea unei abordări inovatoare, transcriptomică unicelulară, și cartografierea rețelelor de comunicare dintre celule pentru principalele tipuri de tumori umane.
2. Echipa va integra de asemenea un număr uriaș de date într-un cadru computațional, pentru a dezvălui vulnerabilitățile celulelor în ecosistemul tumorii și a indica noi ținte pentru refacerea imunității antitumorale.
3. Pentru a evalua semnificația țintelor selectate, echipa AIRC va folosi organoizi, noi modele 3D in vitro care se pot auto-asambla spontan în structuri ordonate care păstrează caracteristici similare cu țesutul uman din care derivă, (cum ar fi eterogenitatea celulară și unele funcții specifice sistemului original), comportându-se asemănător unui avatar.

Fiecare smartphone și desktop Mac care susține Cell Identity Hunter (Faza 2) pe DreamLab va fi un instrument puternic pentru a urmări identitatea celulelor tumorale. Dispozitivul tău va acționa ca un mic avatar al laboratorului, permițând echipei să dobândească cunoștințe superioare despre ecosistemele tumorale și ajutând la identificarea unor tratamente eficace.

# Corona-AI: Shfrytëzimi i fuqisë së AI dhe të mobile supercomputing për përshtatjen e ilaçeve dhe të ushqimeve në luftën kundër koronavirusit

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=Corona-AI%20Albania&body=

5 minute read

Si pjesë e projektit DreamLab: Corona-AI, një ekip i udhëhequr nga Dr Kirill Veselkov nga Departamenti i Kirurgjisë dhe Kancerit në Imperial College London, në bashkëpunim me [Fondacionin Vodafone]( po përdorin teknologji të Inteligjencës Artificiale (AI), superkompjuteri celular dhe të dhëna nga "-omics" për të ngushtuar kërkimin e kombinimeve të barnave ekzistuese dhe molekulave të ngjashme me ilaçet me bazë ushqimore për të ndihmuar në luftën kundër pandemisë koronavirus.

DreamLab tashmë ka përdorur fuqinë përpunuese të mijëra telefonave inteligjentë në status jo-aktiv (idle) për të ndihmuar në zbulimin e ushqimeve dhe ilaçeve të përditshme me veti antikancerogjene (1). Tani kjo fuqi përpunuese po zhvendoset për të ndihmuar luftën kundër koronavirusit.

Shefi i Departamentit të Kirurgjisë dhe Kancerit, Profesori George Hanna, theksoi rëndësinë e projektit:

> **Këto janë periudha të pashembullta, të  cilat kërkojnë zgjidhje radikale dhe të pashembullta shkencore. Ekipi i projektit DreamLab: Corona-AI ka marrë njohuritë dhe suksesin e teknologjisë AI për të zbuluar vetitë antikancerogjene në ilaçe dhe ushqime ekzistuese dhe po e përdor atë kundër këtij kërcënimi të ri global.**

Përhapja e shpejtë e sëmundjes akute të frymëmarrjes (COVID-19) e shkaktuar nga koronavirusi ka pasur një impakt masiv dëmtues në shëndetin e njeriut dhe në ekonominë globale duke çuar në nevojën e menjëhershme të ndërhyrjeve mjekësore dhe ushqyese për të ndihmuar luftën ndaj shpërthimit.

Gjetja e indikacioneve të reja ose përdorimeve alternative të barnave ekzistuese (i njohur si "drug repositioning ") është një mënyrë ndryshe nga procesi i ngadaltë dhe i kushtueshëm i zhvillimit të ilaçeve specifike për të trajtuar këtë sëmundje infektive. Në pandeminë aktuale, ajo mund të shpëtojë mijëra jetë. Dieta e njeriut është e pasur me molekula të ngjashme me ilaçet, të cilat kanë treguar se luajnë një rol edhe në parandalimin dhe trajtimin e sëmundjeve virale, duke bashkëvepruar me ilaçet, ose duke vepruar si "ilaçe". Sidoqoftë, mënyrat tradicionale për hetimin e ndikimit të një ilaçi të vetëm ose të përbërësve ushqimorë do të kërkonim shumë kohë.

Aktualisht, numri i molekulave të mundshme të ngjashme me ilaçet në gjendura në ushqim është shumë i gjerë. Metodologjia tradicionale eksperimentale e hetimit të ndikimit të një ilaçi të vetëm ose të përbërësve ushqimorë në ndonjë infeksion të veçantë viral kërkon muaj ose madje vite.

Projekti DreamLab: Corona-AI merr një qasje rrënjësisht ndryshe ndaj metodave tradicionale të testimit. Mundësuar nga publiku i gjerë, projekti kombinon inteligjencën artificiale dhe fuqinë përpunuese të telefonave inteligjentë për të shpejtuar zbulimin e komponentëve të rinj antiviralë në ilaçet ekzistuese dhe për të ndihmuar në zbulimin e molekulave antivirale në ushqime.

Profesor Vasilis Vasiliou i Shkollës së Shëndetit Publik Yale, anëtar i ekipit DreamLab, theksoi rëndësinë e projektit: "Në këto periudha të vështira, AI mund të bëjë një botë me të mirë në garën kundër Covid-19. Ekipi i Dreamlab është duke përdorur fuqinë e AI për të kontrolluar barnat, të cilat mund të kenë potencialin për të luftuar Covid-19 dhe gjithashtu për të identifikuar përbërësit e ushqimeve të cilat mund të forcojnë imunitetin tonë kundër efekteve të virusit ".

### AI novatore e drejtuar nga rrjeti për ripozicionimin e ilaçeve dhe zbulimin e molekulave anti-virale në ushqime

Koronaviruset nuk mund të mbijetojnë ose të përsëriten pa ndihmën e ushtrive të tyre. Në fakt, të gjitha viruset kanë evoluar në mënyrë natyrale një arsenal të sofistikuar të strategjive molekulare për të mbijetuar dhe riprodhuar. Këto strategji mbështeten në një rrjet kompleks të ndërveprimeve fizike midis proteinave virale dhe atyre pritëse, të ashtuquajturat "rrjete ndërvepruese të virusit" (2).

Modeli tradicional i zhvillimit të ilaçeve anti-virale është ‘një ilaç për çdo proteinë virale. Kjo qasje ka të meta të shumta, ndër të cilat është mutacioni i virusit i cili mund ta bëjë shpejt ilaçin jo efikas ose të padobishëm. Në vend të kësaj, ne duhet të synojmë një ndërveprim me të gjithë virusin. Edhe pse kane avancuar, vaksinat parandaluese dhe efektet e ilaceve kundër SARS-Cov-2, ka të ngjarë që proteina specifike të rrethohen nga gjenerimi i mutantëve viralë.

Duke përfituar nga puna jonë e mëparshme në lidhje me kancer, projekti Corona-AI DreamLab "synon të ndërtojë një panoramë më të qartë të molekulave individuale ose kombinimeve të molekulave më të përshtatshme për të prishur rrjetet ndërvepruese virale molekulare të nevojshme për mbijetesën e koronavirusit, jo vetëm të proteinave të tij specifike ”, sipas Dr. Kirill Veselkov. Këto molekula mund të jenë ose ilaçe ekzistuese (d.m.th. ilaçe që nuk njihen më parë ose ilace të cilat përdoren për trajtime anti-virale) ose molekula të ngjashme me ilaçet brenda ushqimeve. Rezultatet mund të hedhin dritë në terapitë me shumë ilaçe, së bashku me ndërhyrjet dietike.

Një anëtar i skuadrës DreamLab, Profesori Michael Bronstein, Kryetar i Machine Learning dhe Pattern Recognition në Departamentin e Computing Imperial College në Londër dhe Kryetar i Studimit të Mësimit Grafik në Twitter, përshkroi thelbin e algoritmit të projektit:

> **Ne jemi duke përdorur metoda të bazuara në AI për të identifikuar komponimet antivirale mes mijëra molekulave, duke modeluar efektet rrjet të ndërveprimeve midis këtyre molekulave dhe biomolekulave në trupin tonë. Ne i kemi përdorur më parë me sukses këto metoda për të gjetur "hyperfoods" të cilat përmbajnë komponime të ngjashme me ilaçet anti-kancerogjene, dhe tani po i përdorim ato për sëmundjen e re.**

### Natyra kombinuese e problemit: Pse DreamLab?

Progresi i këtij hulumtimi fillimisht u ngadalësua nga mungesa e pajisjeve superkompjuterike. Kombinimet e tre, katër, apo edhe më shumë komponimeve do të ishin të pamundura të testoheshin në laborator. Le të themi se ka 10,000 molekula, me kombinime të ndryshme - kjo është një trilion mundësi që duhet të "testohen" në mënyrë llogjike, diçka që do të ishte e pakontrollueshme në një kompjuter normal. Aplikacioni DreamLab përdor machine learning në një rrjet celular, për të analizuar miliarda kombinime të ilaçeve ekzistuese, molekulave të gjendura në ushqime dhe të ndërveprimeve gjenetike, duke zvogëluar rrënjësisht kohën e nevojshme për të bërë zbulime.

Anëtari i ekipit të projektit, Dr Reza Mirnezami, Këshilltar Kirurg Colorectal, Royal Free Hospital, tha: "Ekipi i DreamLab po punon për të shfrytëzuar fuqinë e AI për të identifikuar se si ilaçet ekzistuese mund të" ripërdoren "në luftën kundër COVID -19.

Gjatë kësaj faze fillestare të projektit Corona-AI, ne synojmë të testojmë kombinime deri në dy molekula të barnave ekzistuese ose molekula të ngjashme me ilaçet në ushqime kundër ndërveprimeve të koronavirus përfshirë edhe SARS-Cov-2 - agjentin përgjegjës për pandeminë aktuale COVID-19. Rrjeti i telefonave inteligjentë DreamLab me 100K përdorues ka fuqinë e kombinuar të një super kompjuteri i cili mund të grumbullojë të dhënat nga kjo fazë në një kohë që përndryshe do të kërkonte një dekadë duke përdorur një kompjuter desktop standard.

Shkarkoni DreamLab tani për të na ndihmuar të luftojmë së bashku COVID-19.

_Autor; Dr Kirill Veselkov; Ligjërues në Mjekësinë Kompjuterike dhe Informatikën e Kancerit, Departamenti i Kirurgjisë dhe Kancerit, Imperial College London; Asistent Profesor Adjunct i Epidemiologjisë, Shkolla e Shëndetit Publik Yale, SHBA._

# Cell Identity Hunter Phase 2 - New Zealand

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=Cell%20Identity%20Hunter%20Phase%202%20-%20New%20Zealand&body=

1 minute read

### What will Phase 2 of Cell Identity Hunter achieve?

Thank you for powering Cell Identity Hunter, Phase 2! The objective of this project is to characterise the tumour ecosystem, specifically the relationship between immune system and tumour cells. This is made possible thanks to the combination of three cutting-edge technologic approaches by the Italian Association for Cancer Research (AIRC):

1. The AIRC team will map the tumour ecosystem by determining the nature of each and every one of its cells. They will do this by adopting an innovative single-cell transcriptomics approach, and will map the communication networks between the cells in the main types of human tumour.
2. The team will also integrate a huge amount of data through an ad hoc computational framework to reveal the cells vulnerabilities in the tumour ecosystem, pointing out new targets to restore anti-tumour immunity.
3. In order to evaluate the significance of the selected targets, the AIRC team will employ organoids, new in vitro 3D models able to spontaneously assemble themselves in ordered structures that preserve similar characteristics to the human tissue they are derived from (such as cellular heterogeneity and some functions specific to the original system), behaving as a kind of avatar.

Every smartphone and mac desktop that powers Cell Identity Hunter (Phase 2) on DreamLab will be a powerful tool to track the identikit of tumour cells. Your device will act as a small avatar of the lab, enabling the team to gain a superior knowledge of tumour ecosystems and help to identify effective cures.

# Aplikacioni Dream Lab në bashkëpunim me Imperial College London bëjnë të mundur përshpejtimin e kerkime shkencore për gjetjen e kurave që mund të ndihmojë potencialisht personat e prekur me COVID-19

9 Dec 2020

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=Aplikacioni%20Dream%20Lab%20me%20%20b%C3%ABjn%C3%AB%20t%C3%AB%20mundur%20p%C3%ABrshpejtimin%20e%20kerkime%20p%C3%ABr%20gjetjen%20e%20kurave%20q%C3%AB%20mund%20t%C3%AB%20ndihmoj%C3%AB%20personat%20e%20prekur%20me%20COVID-19&body=

3 minute read

Përdoruesit e aplikacionit DreamLab kanë ndihmuar Imperial College London të përfundojë Fazën 1 të projektit të tij kërkimor Corona-AI në vetëm gjashtë muaj, duke identifikuar molekulat në ushqimet e përditshme me bazë bimore që kanë veti anti-virale dhe përdorimeve alternative të barnave ekzistuese që mund rekomandohen kundër COVID-19 .

Falë përdoruesve të aplikacionit DreamLab në mbarë boten, Imperial College London të përfundojë fazën 1 të projektit të tij kërkimor Corona-AI në vetëm gjashtë muaj. Kjo është dukshëm më e shpejtë sesa do të ishte e mundur me metodat standarde të hulumtimit.

Studiuesit të cilet po punojnë për këtë projekt në Imperial College London kanë njoftuar gjetjet e hershme nga hulumtimi përfshirë këtu edhe identifikimin e molekulave të ushqimit me përberje antivirale për të vlerësuar se cilat kombinime të ushqyerit mund të ofrojnë disa përfitime mjekësore për të trajtuar Covid-19 si për shembull boronica (vecanërisht boronica) molla, portokalli, limoni, lakra, brokoli, qepa hudhra selino, fasule.

Studimi ka vërtetuar gjithashtu se ilacet ekzistuese të cilat përdoren për të luftuar sëmundjet kardiovaskulare dhe çrregullimet metabolike siç janë simvastatin, atorvastatin dhe metformin-

mund të përdoren si indikacione të reja ose përdorime alternative të barnave ekzistues në luftën kundër Covid-19. Gjetjet tani do të analizohen më tej nga studiuesit, të cilët thonë se është e nevojshme vërtetimi klinik për të vlerësuar se çfarë ndikimi mund të kenë këto molekula.

Dr Kirill Veselkov nga Departamenti i Kirurgjisë dhe Kancerit në Imperial College London dhe udhëheqës i studimit, tha: "Ndërsa është shumë e nevojshme zbulimi i një vaksine për COVID-19 ne po kërkojmë mënyra për të ndihmuar njerëzit të cilët e mbartin këtë virus, duke zvogëluar keshtu kohëzgjatjen ose ashpërsinë e sëmundjes.

Për pacientë të cilët nuk është e nevojshme të marrin trajtim në strukturat spitalore ka një nevojë të domosdoshme për trajtime inovatore dhe me kosto efektive. Ne presim që strategjitë precize të të ushqyerit - të hartuara me Hyperfoods të pasura me phytochemical mund të ofrojnë një zgjidhje të re në këtë drejtim.

**TË KONTRIBUOJMË NË LUFTËN KUNDËR COVID-19**

Projekti kërkimor i Imperial College London’s Corona-AI është bërë i mundur falë përdoruesve në mbarë globin që dhurojnë rregullisht kohë kompjuterike në aplikacionin DreamLab.

Rrjeti i telefonave inteligjentë DreamLab me përdorues në të gjithë botën ka fuqinë e kombinuar të një super kompjuteri i cili në një status jo aktiv mund të përpunojë një koleksion të dhënash shkencore në luftën kundër Covid-19 për një kohë fare të shkurtër.

Ekipi i projektit DreamLab: Corona-AI ka marrë njohuritë dhe suksesin e teknologjisë AI për të zbuluar vetitë anti-kancerogjene në ilaçe dhe ushqime ekzistuese ose Corona-AI Faza 1.

Aplikacioni është i disponueshëm në të gjithë botën dhe aktualisht ka përdorues në 17 vende.

Andrew Dunnett, Drejtor i Fondacionit Vodafone, SDGs, tha, "Ndërsa COVID-19 vazhdon të sfidojë shoqërinë tonë në nje menyre te paprecedente, aplikacioni DreamLab ka mundësuar një komunitet global vullnetaresh - nga klientët e Vodafone te përdoruesit e rrjeteve të tjera - për të bërë një ndryshim. Eshtë një shembull frymëzues i njerëzve dhe teknologjisë që bashkojnë forcat për ta bërë botën një vend më të mirë. "

Gjatë fazës 1, projekti Corona-AI kombinon inteligjencën artificiale dhe fuqinë përpunuese të telefonave inteligjentë për të shpejtuar zbulimin e komponentëve të rinj antiviralë në ilaçet ekzistuese dhe për të ndihmuar në zbulimin e molekulave antivirale në ushqime.

Kur të përfundojnë të gjitha fazat e kërkimit - vlerësohet të jetë dhjetori 2021 - rezultatet do të vihen në dispozicion të komunitetit mjekësor për të lehtësuar provat klinike. Gjetjet e lidhura me ushqimin potencialisht mund të përkthehen në këshilla dietike për pacientët që shërohen nga COVID-19.

Për më shumë informacion mbi DreamLab dhe projektin Corona-AI, ju lutemi shkarkoni aplikacionin DreamLab.

# DreamLab app versnelt onderzoek naar medicijnen en ‘Hyperfoods’ voor COVID-19

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=DreamLab%20app%20versnelt%20onderzoek%20voor%20COVID-19&body=

2 minute read

Fase 1 van het Corona-AI-onderzoeksproject is aanzienlijk sneller afgerond dan met standaard onderzoeksmethoden mogelijk zou zijn. Wetenschappers verbonden aan het Imperial College London, hebben de eerste bevindingen van het onderzoek bekendgemaakt, waaronder de identificatie van moleculen met antivirale eigenschappen in plantaardig voedsel zoals bessen (vooral zwarte bessen, veenbessen en bosbessen), appels, sinaasappels, citroenen, kool, broccoli, uien, knoflook, peterselie en bonen.

Uit het onderzoek is ook gebleken dat veelgebruikte geneesmiddelen die worden gebruikt om cardiovasculaire en stofwisselingsstoornissen te bestrijden - zoals simvastatine, atorvastatine en metformine - mogelijk ook werken voor COVID-19. De bevindingen worden nu verder geanalyseerd door de onderzoekers. In deze volgende fase is klinische validatie nodig om te beoordelen welke impact deze moleculen kunnen hebben.

Dr. Kirill Veselkov van het Department of Surgery and Cancer van Imperial College London, die het Corona-AI-project leidt, zegt: “Hoewel er terecht veel aandacht is voor het vinden van een vaccin voor COVID-19, zoeken we ook naar manieren om mensen te behandelen die het virus hebben opgelopen, waardoor de duur of de ernst van de ziekte afneemt. Ook voor COVID-19 patiënten die niet naar het ziekenhuis hoeven, is er behoefte aan goede behandeling. We verwachten dat specifieke voedingsstrategieën - ontworpen met fytochemisch rijke Hyperfoods - een oplossing kunnen bieden om sneller te genezen. "

**Helpen bij het bestrijden van COVID-19**

Het Corona-AI-onderzoeksproject van Imperial College London is mogelijk gemaakt dankzij DreamLab-gebruikers over de hele wereld. DreamLab gebruikt de collectieve rekenkracht van smartphones om samen een virtuele supercomputer te creëren. Zo kunnen duizenden berekeningen worden uitgevoerd, waarvan de resultaten worden teruggestuurd naar het onderzoeksteam.

Sinds april hebben DreamLab-gebruikers de mogelijkheid om het kankeronderzoeksproject “Medicijnen (fase 4)” van Imperial College London te steunen, dat verbanden onderzoekt tussen specifieke medicijn- en voedselmoleculen en genomische netwerken voor kanker, of het “Corona-AI (fase 1)” project. Tot nu toe hebben bijna 1 miljoen mensen in 17 landen DreamLab gedownload.

Louise Meijer, voorzitter van de Vodafone Nederland Foundation: "Het is fantastisch dat zoveel mensen via DreamLab helpen met het versnellen van cruciaal medisch onderzoek. We willen als Foundation met technologische vooruitgang bijdragen aan een betere toekomst en dat we dit samen met onze klanten en andere smartphone-gebruikers kunnen doen, is extra bijzonder. Iedereen voelt de behoefte om bij te dragen en dat kan op een simpele, doeltreffende en veilige manier met DreamLab.”

Tijdens fase 1 heeft het Corona-AI-project duizenden bestaande medicijnen en voedselmoleculen onderzocht met behulp van kunstmatige intelligentie. Er werden 100 miljoen wiskundige berekeningen gemaakt om wetenschappers te helpen de potentiële effecten op COVID-19 te onderzoeken.

Wanneer alle fasen van het onderzoek zijn voltooid – de verwachting is in december 2021 - zullen de resultaten beschikbaar worden gesteld aan de medische wetenschap om klinische proeven te vergemakkelijken. Voedsel gerelateerde bevindingen kunnen mogelijk worden vertaald in voedingsadviezen voor patiënten die herstellen van COVID-19.

Voor meer informatie over DreamLab en het Corona-AI-project, ga naar: [www.vodafone.nl/dreamlab](

# Covid-19: DreamLab completa la Fase 1 de su proyecto Corona-AI

![](

- [facebook](
- [linkedin](
- [twitter](
- [email](mailto:?&subject=DreamLab%20completa%20la%20Fase%201%20de%20su%20proyecto%20Corona-AI&body=

3 minute read

Los países de todo el mundo están suavizando lentamente de las restricciones por el coronavirus, pero la amenaza de la pandemia global está lejos de terminar.

Los científicos están investigando todos los tratamientos posibles para combatir el Covid-19, y es aquí donde entra en juego su Smartphone. Con la aplicación DreamLab de la Fundación Vodafone, su teléfono podría acelerar drásticamente este importante trabajo.

DreamLab funciona utilizando el poder de procesamiento colectivo que tienen los smartphones cuando están inactivos para analizar datos complejos, lo que ayuda a acelerar esta vital investigación para el equipo de científicos del Imperial College de Londres. Un ordenador de escritorio funcionando las 24 horas al día tardaría décadas en procesar los datos, pero una red de 100.000 smartphones que funcionan durante la noche puede hacer el trabajo en solo un par de meses.

Gracias a nuestros usuarios de DreamLab, la Fase 1 del proyecto Corona-AI ya está completa. Desde que se lanzó el proyecto en abril, nuestros usuarios de DreamLab han hecho casi 100 millones de cálculos.

La rapidez y la magnitud de esta investigación ha sido posibles gracias a unos 140.000 dispositivos que han completado alrededor de 1,3 millones de cálculos cada noche con DreamLab. Eso es aproximadamente 10 cálculos por noche por dispositivo, una potencia de procesamiento en DreamLab varias veces superior a la del superordenador del Imperial College.

Corona-AI Phase 1 - 100 millones de cálculos

140.000 teléfonos procesan 1,3 millones de cálculos cada noche

140.000 teléfonos = 3 superordenadores

Esta fase se ha centrado en examinar miles de fármacos y moléculas de alimentos existentes, e identificar aquellos con propiedades antivirales, que podrían usarse para tratar a pacientes con coronavirus.

El proyecto utiliza inteligencia artificial (IA) para comprender qué efectos podrían tener los fármacos y las moléculas alimentarias existentes en el perfil genético de Covid-19 en simulaciones matemáticas. La IA selecciona qué medicamentos y alimentos examinar basándose en los conocimientos de los investigadores y también en sus cálculos sobre cuáles tienen más probabilidades de éxito.

Ahora que la Fase 1 del proyecto Corona-AI está completa, Imperial College publicará estos hallazgos iniciales y luego comenzará la Fase 2. Mientras que la Fase 1 analizó los efectos de los medicamentos individuales y las moléculas de alimentos en Covid-19, la Fase 2 buscará optimizar las combinaciones de estos medicamentos y alimentos para mejorar la eficiencia contra la enfermedad.

Con ambas fases, dependerá de los investigadores del laboratorio replicar cualquier hallazgo prometedor de las simulaciones matemáticas de Corona-AI en el mundo real. Solo entonces, se podrían desarrollar más tratamientos.

El Dr. Kirill Veselkov, científico principal del proyecto en el Imperial College de Londres, enfatizó la importancia de desarrollar tratamientos, incluso si se desarrolla una vacuna eficaz.

“Cualquier tratamiento potencial desarrollado a partir de este proyecto complementaría una vacuna, no la reemplazaría”, dijo el Dr. Veselkov. “Especialmente si el virus muta y se adapta, permaneciendo con nosotros en los próximos años”.

Este trabajo será posible si todos conectamos nuestros Smartphones (y tablets) todas las noches y alimentamos la aplicación DreamLab. Disponible para iOS y Android, se puede descargar de forma gratuita desde la [App Store]( y [Google Play](

# Capital allocation

In March 2024, we conducted a broad capital allocation review, considering the Group's strategy within its reshaped footprint. Following an extensive review of our capital investment requirements, the current capital intensity will be broadly maintained by market, which we believe allows for appropriate investment in networks and growth opportunities. A new leverage policy of $2 . 2 5 \\times$ – 2.75x Net Debt to Adjusted EBITDAaL will be adopted and we will target to operate within the bottom half of this range. The new leverage policy supports a solid investment grade credit rating and positions Vodafone to continue to invest for growth over the long-term.

Following the right-sizing of the portfolio as a result of the sale of Vodafone Spain and Vodafone Italy, the Board has determined to adopt a new rebased dividend from FY25 onwards. The Board is targeting a dividend of 4.5c per share for FY25, with an ambition to grow it over time. The new dividend has been set at a sustainable level, which ensures appropriate cash flow cover and sufficient flexibility to invest in the business for growth. The Board has also approved a capital return through share buybacks of up to $$ 20$ billion of proceeds from the sale of Vodafone Spain. The Board anticipates the opportunity for further share buybacks of up to $\\yen 20$ billion following the completion of the sale of Vodafone Italy, which is expected in the first half of 2025.

Accordingly, Vodafone Spain and Vodafone Italy are excluded from the results of continuing operations and are instead presented as a single amount as a loss after tax from discontinued operations in the Group’s consolidated income statement. Discontinued operations are also excluded from the Group’s segment reporting. # Growth

Total revenue decreased by $1 . 2 %$ to $\\yen 123.0$ billion, driven by lower equipment revenue. Service revenue grew by $0 . 2 %$ (Q3: $0 . 3 %$ Q4: $0 . 6 %$ as the contribution from higher broadband ARPU was largely offset by the cumulative impact of broadband and TV customer losses and lower regulated rates for terminating mobile calls. Growth improved in Q4, as higher consumer mobile ARPU and customer base growth was partially offset by a 0.9 percentage point impact from the end to bulk TV contracting in Multi Dwelling Units (‘MDUs’).

Fixed service revenue increased by $0 . 3 %$ (Q3: $1 . 0 %$ , Q4: $- 0 . 2 %$ ) as broadband ARPU growth was partially offset by the impact of a lower broadband and TV customer base. The slowdown in fixed service revenue growth in Q4 was primarily driven by a 1.7 percentage point impact from changes to German TV laws. Mobile service revenue was stable year-on-year (Q3: $.0 . 5 %$ , Q4: $+ 1 . 8 %$ as ARPU growth and higher roaming and visitor revenue were offset by a lower prepaid customer base and a reduction in mobile termination rates. Mobile service revenue growth in Q4 improved having lapped the renewal and rephasing of a large multi-year IoT contract last year, which had adversely impacted prior quarters. Mobile service revenue growth in $\\mathtt { Q 4 }$ was also supported by higher IoT project revenue, consumer contract ARPU growth, and a higher customer base. Vodafone Business service revenue was stable year-on-year (Q3: - $1 . 9 %$ , Q4: $+ 1 . 0 % )$ as good demand for fixed services, including cloud and security, was offset by a strong prior year performance in public sector and lower IoT revenue following the renewal of a major multi-year IoT automotive contract in the prior year.

Adjusted EBITDAaL declined by $5 . 8 %$ , reflecting a 2.7 percentage point impact from higher energy costs. The decline also reflected higher wage, inflation-linked lease costs, and customer acquisition costs, as well as investments made to support the MDU transition. The Adjusted EBITDAaL margin was 1.9 percentage points lower year-on-year at $3 8 . 7 %$ .

# Customers

During the year, we re-engineered our commercial model and launched a number of new products and services to better serve our customers. In broadband, we restored our market leading network quality position. This was reflected in four major independent network test results from Connect, CHIP, Computer BILD and nPerf where we achieved leading quality and reliability scores. Reflecting inflationary pressure, we have increased the price of our broadband packages. As expected, this impacted our commercial performance with our broadband customer base declining by 392,000 during the year. Our converged customer base increased by 99,000 to 2.4 million.

Ahead of changes to German TV laws, which take effect from July 2024 and change the practice of bulk TV contracting in MDUs, we have started migrating end users to new contracts at scale. Based on our experience to date, we expect to retain around $50 %$ of the 8.5 million MDU TV households. At the end of March 2024, we had already actively retained 1.9 million households. Our total TV customer base declined by 1.0 million during the year, primarily due to the MDU transition, which began in the last quarter of FY24.

We added 239,000 new mobile contract customers in FY24, supported by our new propositions, the ongoing optimisation of sales channels and an improved performance of Vodafone’s own brands. We also added 8.0 million IoT connections, driven by continued strong demand from the automotive sector. During the year, we agreed a long-term national roaming partnership with 1&1. We expect to deliver mobile coverage nationwide to 1&1's customers from the second half of the 2024 calendar year. Our fibre-tothe-home (‘FTTH’) joint venture, OXG Glasfaser, started its network rollout during the year, initially in Neuss, Düsseldorf, Marburg and Kassel. OXG Glasfaser will deploy FTTH to up to seven million homes over a six-year period and is complementary to our upgrade plans for our existing hybrid fibre cable network.

Total revenue increased by $0 . 2 %$ to $\\yen 6.8$ billion as service revenue growth was offset by a decline in equipment revenue. Service revenue increased by $5 . 1 %$ (Q3: $5 . 5 %$ , Q4: $6 . 8 %$ . The lower service revenue growth in Q4 was driven by Business following strong project revenue in prior periods.

Mobile service revenue grew by $5 . 4 % ( 0 3 ; 5 . 8 % , 0 4 ; 6 . 8 % )$ . Organic growth in mobile service revenue was $5 . 4 % ( { \\sf Q } 3 ; 5 . 4 % , { \\sf Q } 4 ; 3 . 7 % ) ,$ , driven by good commercial momentum, annual price increases, and higher roaming revenue, partially offset by the migration of the Virgin Media MVNO off our network. The lower growth in Q4 was driven by a strong Business performance in prior periods. Fixed service revenue grew by $4 . 1 %$ (Q3: $4 . 6 %$ , Q4: $7 . 0 %$ . Organic growth in fixed service revenue was $3 . 9 %$ (Q3: $4 . 6 %$ , Q4: $3 . 5 %$ driven by good Consumer customer base growth.

Vodafone Business service revenue increased by $3 . 3 %$ (Q3: $6 . 3 %$ , Q4: $2 . 6 %$ . Organic growth in Vodafone Business service revenue was $3 . 2 %$ (Q3: $5 . 8 %$ , Q4: $- 0 . 5 %$ , due to strong growth in mobile supported by annual price increases. Growth was also supported by our IoT business and during the year, we announced we will be providing IoT connectivity to Britain’s smart metering network through our partnership with Data Communications Company ('DCC'). Fixed sales momentum continued to improve throughout the year. We also announced a new channel called Business IT Hubs, which is planning to establish 300 franchise partners to help SMEs better manage their IT solutions.

Adjusted EBITDAaL increased by $4 . 3 %$ in the year. On an organic basis, Adjusted EBITDAaL increased by $4 . 0 %$ , with strong service revenue growth, partially offset by a 1.8 percentage point impact from higher energy costs, and the migration of the Virgin Media MVNO off our network. The adjusted EBITDAaL margin improved by 0.8 percentage points year-on-year on a reported and organic basis to $20 %$ .

# Customers

During the year our mobile contract customer base continued to grow, however this was offset by low value disconnections in Business. In the second half of the year, we were recognised as a Consumer NPS co-leader in the market and we are now the joint lowest complained about mobile operator, as measured by Ofcom, reflecting the significant improvements and investment we have made to our customer experience over the last few years. Our digital prepaid sub-brand ‘VOXI’ continued to grow, with 120,000 customers added during the year. Through our partnerships with CityFibre and Openreach we can now reach 15.3 million households with full fibre broadband, more than any other provider in the UK. We are one of the fastest growing broadband providers in the UK and our broadband customer base increased by 160,000.

# Portfolio

In June 2023, we announced a binding agreement to combine our UK business with Three UK to create a sustainable, and competitive third scaled network operator in the UK. Following the merger, which we expect to close around the end of the 2024 calendar year, Vodafone and CK Hutchison will own $51 %$ and $49 %$ of the combined business, respectively. This combination is expected to provide customers with greater choice and more value, drive greater competition, and enable increased investment with a clear £11 billion plan to create one of Europe’s most advanced standalone 5G networks. Full details of the transaction can be found here: investors.vodafone.com/merger-of-vodafone-uk-and-three-uk.

Total revenue declined by $4 . 2 %$ to $\\mathtt { \\Omega } \_ { \\mathtt { C } 5 . 5 } ^ { \\mathtt { \\Gamma } }$ billion, reflecting the disposal of Vodafone Hungary in the prior year. Service revenue decreased by $5 . 7 %$ (Q3: - $78 %$ , Q4: $+ 0 . 3 %$ . In Portugal, both our Consumer and Business segments continued to perform well, also supported by inflation-linked contractual price increases implemented in March 2023. Adjusted EBITDAaL decreased by $7 . 1 %$ in the year. # Customers

During FY24, we maintained our good commercial momentum. In Portugal, we added 167,000 mobile contract customers and 58,000 fixed broadband customers. In Ireland, our mobile contract customers base increased by 30,000. Through our fixed wholesale network access partnerships, we now cover over 1.4 million households in Ireland with FTTH. In Greece, we added 146,000 mobile contract customers, and our broadband customer base declined by 12,000.

# Portfolio

In September 2022, we announced that we had entered into an agreement to buy Portugal’s fourth largest converged operator, Nowo Communications, from Llorca JVCO Limited, the owner of Masmovil Ibercom S.A. The transaction is conditional on regulatory approval. We submitted proposed remedies which were rejected in early 2024. After reviewing the competition authority’s comments and exploring further options to address the authority’s concerns, we submitted revised proposals that are currently being considered by the competition authority.

# Growth

Total revenue declined by $8 . 1 %$ to $\\pm 7 . 4$ billion due to the depreciation of local currencies versus the euro. Service revenue decreased by $9 . 2 %$ (Q3: - $. 7 . 5 %$ , $Q 4 : + 1 . 2 % )$ . Organic growth in service revenue grew by $9 . 2 %$ (Q3: $8 . 8 %$ , $\\mathtt { Q 4 }$ : $10 . 0 %$ with growth in South Africa, Egypt and Vodacom’s international markets.

In South Africa, service revenue growth was supported by the Consumer mobile contract segment, which benefited from a price increase in the first quarter, and good fixed line growth in Consumer and Business. Growth slowed in Q4 due to a strong prior year comparative, reflecting an acceleration in customer data usage during widespread power outages, and pressure on wholesale revenue. Financial services revenue grew by $7 . 9 %$ to €156.9 million on an organic basis, supported by growth in our insurance services.

Service revenue in Egypt grew strongly during the year and accelerated in Q4, above inflation. The acceleration was supported by sustained customer base growth, price increases in mobile and fixed, robust demand for data and strong growth in our financial services product, ‘Vodafone Cash’. Vodafone Cash revenue more than doubled in FY24 to €95.8 million.

In Vodacom’s international markets, service revenue growth was supported by a higher customer base and strong M-Pesa and data revenue growth. M-Pesa revenue grew by $1 3 . 4 %$ on an organic basis and now represents $2 6 . 8 %$ of service revenue.

Adjusted EBITDAaL declined by $11 . 8 %$ during the year. On an organic basis, adjusted EBITDAaL increased by $6 . 4 %$ , supported by service revenue growth and cost initiatives, partially offset by an increase in technology operating expenses associated with higher energy costs. The Adjusted EBITDAaL margin decreased by 1.5 percentage points year-on-year (organic: -1.1 percentage points) to $34 . 2 %$ .

# Customers

In South Africa, we added 125,000 contract customers in the year, and now have a mobile contract base of 6.8 million. We added 5.7 million mobile prepaid SIMs in the year, supported by our big data led customer value management capabilities which offer personalised bundles to customers. Across our active customer base, $81 . 5 %$ of our mobile customers now use data services, an increase of 3.3 million year-on-year. Our ‘VodaPay’ super-app continued to gain traction with 5.8 million registered users.

In Egypt, we added 437,000 contract customers and 2.4 million prepaid mobile customers during the year, and we now have 48.3 million customers. ‘Vodafone Cash’ now has 8.2 million active users with 2.8 million users added during the year.

In Vodacom’s international markets, we added 4.0 million mobile customers and our mobile customer base is now 54.2 million, with $67 . 5 %$ of active customers using our data services.

Further information on our operations in Africa can be accessed here: vodacom.com.

# Vantage Towers – $5 3 . 9 %$ ownership

On 23 March 2023, we announced the completion of Oak Holdings GmbH, our co-control partnership for Vantage Towers with a consortium of long-term infrastructure investors led by Global Infrastructure Partners and KKR. We received initial net proceeds of $$ 4.9$ billion in March 2023, and a further €500 million in July 2023, taking total net proceeds to $\\pm 5 . 4$ billion and the Consortium's ownership in Oak Holdings GmbH to $40 %$ . During the year, total revenue increased $6 . 3 %$ to €1.1 billion, driven by 2,400 net new tenancies and 1,100 new macro sites. As a result, the tenancy ratio increased to $1 . 5 0 \\times$ . Vodafone’s share of results in FY24 reflects the amortisation of intangible assets arising from the completion of the co-control partnership for Vantage Towers. During the year, Vodafone received €196 million in dividends from Vantage Towers.

# VodafoneZiggo Joint Venture (Netherlands) – $50 . 0 %$ ownership

The results of VodafoneZiggo are prepared under US GAAP, which is broadly consistent with Vodafone’s IFRS basis of reporting. Total revenue increased $1 . 5 %$ to $\\pm 4 . 1$ billion, as contractual price increases and mobile contract customer growth were partially offset by a decline in the fixed customer base. During the year, VodafoneZiggo added 129,000 mobile contract customers. VodafoneZiggo’s broadband customer base declined by 115,000 customers to 3.2 million due to the competitive price environment. The number of converged households increased by 20,000 and $48 %$ of broadband customers are now converged, delivering significant NPS and customer loyalty benefits. VodafoneZiggo now offers gigabit speeds to 7.5 million homes, providing nationwide coverage. Vodafone’s lower share of results in FY24 was largely due to lower adjusted EBITDA, lower gains on derivative financial instruments and higher third-party interest expenses. During the year, Vodafone received €100 million in equity distributions and €51 million in interest payments from the joint venture.

Adjusted effective tax rate is a non-GAAP measure. See page 36 for more information.

The Group’s effective tax rate (‘ETR’) for the year ended 31 March 2024 was $3 . 1 %$ , (FY23: $3 . 8 %$ . The rate remains low following the recognition of a €1,019 million deferred tax asset on losses in Luxembourg as a result of favourable case law during the year. The ETR also reflects a tax credit of €249 million (2023: €309 million) relating to the impacts of inflation in Turkey.

The year ended 31 March 2023 included gains on the disposals of Vantage Towers and Vodafone Ghana which were largely exempt from tax, except for a €88 million charge relating to the disposal of Vantage Towers, as well as the hyperinflation accounting impacts in Turkey and utilisation of losses in Luxembourg.

The Group’s Adjusted ETR (‘AETR’) for the year ended 31 March 2024 was $24 . 5 %$ (FY23: $2 5 . 6 %$ . The AETR excludes the recognition of a deferred tax asset in Luxembourg, the impact of a €598 million tax charge (2023: €33 million) relating to the use of losses in Luxembourg and the effects of inflation in Turkey.

The charge on losses in Luxembourg is higher than the prior year because of an internal restructuring in 2023 which resulted in a loss. As a result of that restructuring, profits in Luxembourg are no longer subject to changes in the value of investments. The effects of hyperinflation accounting in Turkey, and the tax charge relating to the disposal of Vantage Towers in 2023, are set out above.

The main drivers for the reduction in the AETR are the mix of profits between jurisdictions in 2024 compared to 2023 and Vodafone Spain moving to discontinued operations accounting in 2024 as previously the non-recognition of tax losses in Spain increased AETR.

# Return on capital employed

Return on capital employed (‘ROCE’) reflects how efficiently we are generating profit with the capital we deploy. We calculate two ROCE measures: i) Pre-tax ROCE for controlled operations only and ii) Post-tax ROCE including associates and joint ventures. ROCE calculated using GAAP measures for the 12 months ended 31 March 2024 was $3 . 4 %$ (FY23: $1 3 . 0 % )$ , impacted by gains on disposal in the prior year of Vantage Towers A.G. and Vodafone Ghana, partially offset by the loss on the disposal of Vodafone Hungary.

# Dividends

Dividends will continue to be declared in euros, aligning the Group’s shareholder returns with the primary currency in which we generate free cash flow, and paid in euros, pounds sterling and US dollars. The foreign exchange rate at which future dividends declared in euros will be converted into pounds sterling and US dollars will be calculated based on the average World Markets Company benchmark rates over the five business days during the week prior to the payment of the dividend.

The Board is recommending total dividends per share of 9.0 eurocents for the year. This includes a final dividend of 4.5 eurocents compared to 4.5 eurocents in the prior year.

The ex-dividend date for the final dividend is 6 June 2024 for ordinary shareholders and 7 June 2024 for ADR holders, the record date is 7 June 2024 and the dividend is payable on 2 August 2024. Shareholders may elect to receive their dividend in either eurocents or GBP and the last day for election will be 12 July 2024. Alternatively, shareholders may participate in the dividend reinvestment plan and elections must be made by 12 July 2024. More information can be found at vodafone.com/dividends

# Other significant developments

# Executive Committee changes

The following changes were effective from 1 April 2024:

Ahmed Essam was appointed Executive Chairman Vodafone Germany and CEO European markets. Ahmed Essam was previously CEO Vodafone UK and remains a member of the Executive Committee.

Serpil Timuray, previously CEO Europe Cluster, was appointed CEO Vodafone Investments and is responsible for the Group’s joint ventures, partner markets and the development of new telecom partnerships. Serpil remains a member of the Executive Committee.

Philippe Rogge, previously CEO Vodafone Germany and member of the Executive Committee, decided to step down and leave Vodafone.

Marika Auramo has been appointed CEO of Vodafone Business and a member of the Executive Committee, effective 1 July 2024.

# Other leadership changes

Max Taylor, previously Chief Commercial Officer of Vodafone UK, has been appointed CEO of Vodafone UK.

Marcel de Groot, previously Consumer Business Director, has been appointed CEO of Vodafone Germany.

# Notes to the condensed consolidated financial statements

# 1 Basis of preparation

The preliminary results for the year ended 31 March 2024 are an abridged statement of the full Annual Report which was approved by the Board of Directors on 14 May 2024. The consolidated financial statements in the full Annual Report are prepared in accordance with UK-adopted International Financial Reporting Standards (‘IFRS’), with IFRS as issued by the International Accounting Standards Board (‘IASB’) and with the requirements of the Companies Act 2006.

The auditor’s report on those consolidated financial statements was unqualified, did not draw attention to any matters by way of emphasis without qualifying their report and did not contain statements under section 498(2) or 498(3) of the Companies Act 2006. The preliminary results do not comprise statutory accounts within the meaning of section 434(3) of the Companies Act 2006. The Annual Report for the year ended 31 March 2024 will be delivered to the Registrar of Companies following the Company’s Annual General Meeting on 30 July 2024.

The financial information included in this preliminary announcement does not itself contain sufficient information to comply with IFRS. A separate announcement will be made in accordance with Disclosure and Transparency Rules (DTR) 6.3 when the Annual Report for the year ended 31 March 2024 is made available on the Company’s website in June 2024.

The preparation of the preliminary results requires management to make estimates and assumptions that affect the reported amounts of assets and liabilities, disclosure of contingent assets and liabilities at the end of the reporting period and the reported amounts of revenue and expenses during the reporting period. Actual results could vary from these estimates. The estimates and underlying assumptions are reviewed on an ongoing basis. Revisions to accounting estimates are recognised in the period in which the estimate is revised if the revision affects only that period or in the period of the revision and future periods if the revision affects both current and future periods.

# Going concern

The Group has $\\mathtt { \\Pi } \_ { \\mathtt { \\overline { { r } } 6 . 1 } }$ billion of cash and cash equivalents available as at 31 March 2024 which, together with undrawn revolving credit facilities of $\\yen 7.8$ billion, cover all of the Group’s reasonably expected cash requirements over the going concern period. The Directors have reviewed trading and liquidity forecasts for the Group, which were based on current trading conditions, and considered a variety of scenarios. In addition to the liquidity forecasts prepared, the Directors considered the availability of the Group’s revolving credit facilities which were undrawn as at 31 March 2024. As a result of the assessment performed, the Directors have concluded that the Group is able to continue in operation for a period of at least 12 months from the date of approving the consolidated financial statements and that it is appropriate to continue to adopt the going concern basis in preparing the consolidated financial statements.

# Critical accounting judgements and estimates

The Group’s critical accounting judgements and estimates are disclosed in the Group’s Annual Report for the year ended 31 March 2024.

# New accounting pronouncements adopted

On 1 April 2023, the Group adopted certain new accounting policies where necessary to comply with amendments to IFRS, none of which had a material impact on the consolidated results, financial position or cash flows of the Group. Further details are provided in the Group’s Annual Report for the prior year ended 31 March 2023.

# Notes to the condensed consolidated financial statements

# 1 Basis of preparation (continued)

Hyperinflation accounting

The Group continues to apply hyperinflationary accounting, as specified in IAS 29, at its Turkish operations whose functional currency is the Turkish lira and to Safaricom’s operations in Ethiopia where the Ethiopian birr is the functional currency.

Turkish lira and Ethiopian birr results and non-monetary asset and liability balances for the year ended 31 March 2024 have been revalued to their present value equivalent local currency amounts as at 31 March 2024, based on an inflation index, before translation to euros at the reporting date exchange rate of €1:34.94 TRL and €1:61.43 ETB, respectively.

For the Group’s operations in Turkey, the gain or loss on net monetary assets resulting from IAS 29 application is recognised in the consolidated income statement within Other income.

For Safaricom’s operations in Ethiopia, the impacts of IAS 29 accounting are reflected as an adjustment to Investments in associates and joint ventures and to Profit attributable to the joint ventures and associates.

The inflation index in Turkey selected to reflect the change in purchasing power was the consumer price index (‘CPI’) issued by the Turkish Statistical Institute which has risen by $6 8 . 5 0 %$ during the year ended 31 March 2024. The inflation index selected in Ethiopia is the CPI issued by the Central Statistics Agency of Ethiopia which rose $2 6 . 2 0 %$ during the year ended 31 March 2024.

The main impacts of these adjustments on the consolidated financial statements are shown below.

# 3 Discontinued operations and assets held for sale

# Discontinued operations

Where operations constitute a separately reportable segment and have been disposed of, or are classified as held for sale, the Group classifies such operations as discontinued.

Discontinued operations are excluded from the results of continuing operations and are presented as a single amount as profit or loss after tax from discontinued operations in the Consolidated income statement. Discontinued operations are also excluded from segment reporting. All other notes to the Condensed consolidated financial statements include amounts for continuing operations, unless indicated otherwise.

Transactions between the Group's continuing and discontinued operations are eliminated in full in the Consolidated income statement. To the extent that the Group considers that the commercial relationships with discontinued operations will continue post-disposal, transactions are reflected within continuing operations with an opposite charge or credit reflected within the results of discontinued operations resulting in a net nil impact on the Group’s Profit for the financial year for the years presented.

On 31 October 2023, the Group announced that it had entered into binding agreements with Zegona Communications plc (’Zegona’) in relation to the sale of $100 %$ of Vodafone Holdings Europe, S.L.U. (‘Vodafone Spain’). The expected completion of the disposal is the first half of 2024.

On 15 March 2024, the Group announced that it had entered into a binding agreement with Swisscom AG (‘Swisscom’) in relation to the sale of $100 %$ of Vodafone Italia S.p.A. (’Vodafone Italy’). The expected completion of the disposal is in the first half of 2025.

Consequently, the results of Vodafone Spain and Vodafone Italy are reported as discontinued operations and the assets and iabilities of both are presented as held for sale in the consolidated statement of financial position.

The consideration for Vodafone Spain is comprised of €4.1 billion cash to be paid on completion and non-cash consideration with a nominal value of $\\mathtt { \\Omega } \_ { \\mathtt { c } 0 . 9 }$ billion. The non-cash consideration comprises Redeemable Preference Shares (‘RPS’) which will be issued to Vodafone by a newly created entity, which will subscribe for new ordinary shares in Zegona for an amount, based on the issue price for Zegona's equity raise, that is equivalent to the amount of RPS being subscribed for by Vodafone. The RPS will be redeemed 6 years after completion, or earlier following a material liquidity event or exit for Zegona that releases funds to its shareholders. A proportion of the consideration isrelated to futureservices tobe provided by the Group toZegona. For the year ended 31 March 2024, the Grouprecorded a non-cash charge of €345 million (pre and post-tax), included in discontinued operations, as a result of the re-measurement of Vodafone Spain to its fair value less costs to sell. The charge mostly results from the non-recognition of €538 million (pre and post-tax) depreciation and amortisation of non-current assets from the date Vodafone Spain was classified as held for sale.

The fair value of the Group’s equity interest at 31 March 2024 was determined with reference to the consideration expected from the agreed sale to Zegona less adjustments for estimated completion adjustments, consideration for future services to be received by Zegona from the Group and the elimination of intercompany debt. This approach was considered to result in a level 2 valuation in accordance with IFRS 13 as certain estimated completion adjustments and the fair value of the non-cash consideration, are not observable.
